notes: A long agentic turn carrying MANY sequential tool calls in ONE turn, validating that state tracking stays coherent across the burst: the session stays continuously working (no flicker to ready or waiting between successive function_call/function_call_output pairs), the turn settles cleanly to ready, and the session is never stuck. Unlike the claudecode recipe for this scenario — which has to use 4 plain prompts because modern claudecode refuses auto-mode for paid models and stalls tool-call turns at a permission gate — codex has NO permission gate (capabilities.json permission_hooks:false), so it runs the real many-tool-call single-turn shape natively. The codex recipe drives ONE interactive prompt that makes codex explore a directory: pwd, then ls -la, then wc -l on the first few files, then summarize. The codex parser models each tool call as a paired function_call/function_call_output (open/close); a completed command's output CLOSES its open call (HasOpenToolCall clears) so the daemon never treats an intermediate result as a waiting gate, and task_complete→turn_done is the only ready settle (parser.go lines 155-170), suppressing the intermediate assistant_message flicker. So the daemon observes: proc-PID pre-session born ready, the rollout session reconcile (new session_id) born ready, ONE ready→working at the prompt, ONE contiguous working span across the whole burst, ONE working→ready at task_complete, process_exited at REPL teardown. The burst_working dwell (duration_at_least_ms 4000) with no-ready/no-waiting invariants is the load-bearing no-flicker assertion: if any intermediate tool result flickered the session to ready or waiting mid-burst, this phase would fire. The DSL has no command-count form, so the minimum-tool-call assertion lives in the recipe verify and the transcript's function_call pairs, not as a phase. Exact command count, tool output text, and token totals vary run-to-run (model/OS-dependent) and are NOT asserted.

text: Pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch
Rollout session reconciles (different session_id), born ready
PID bound to the rollout session
User prompt → working on the rollout session (start of the agentic burst)
State stays continuously working across the many sequential tool calls — no flicker to ready or waiting between successive function_call/function_call_output pairs in the same turn
After the final tool call and summary the turn completes → back to ready; state never entered waiting during the burst
Driver tears down the REPL — process_exited on the rollout session
